title: Measure fallback routes and nightly diagnosis in Vibe-Lab
goal: Run comparable Vibe-Lab experiments over deterministic Grabowski work, local AI, Gemini Flash via AGY and quality-escalation backends, and evaluate whether the nightly diagnosis loop improves decisions without excessive cost, noise or false confidence.

acceptance.assertion: Runs bind task class, risk, context size, baseline commit, expected checks and diff-size band so cheaper and stronger backends are not compared on materially different work.

Each route records marginal monetary cost where available, local compute time, elapsed time, retries, patch size, validation result, review findings, rework, escaped defects and operator intervention.
Agent-authored tests or self-assessment are not independent proof; evaluation includes existing tests, CI, diff-bound review, operator notes or patch-evaluation evidence.

The nightly cycle is scored for actionable unique findings, duplicate/noise rate, false positives, missed known failures, runtime, resource use and whether the morning digest changes an operator decision.
The experiment concludes with promote, pilot, defer or drop per task class and route, preserving uncertainty and an off-ramp rather than declaring one universal best backend.
No executor order, schedule or mutation authority changes automatically from Vibe-Lab output; promotion requires a reviewed registry or policy change.
